findings.evidence: Existing symmetry API provides `@[simp] theorem mem_OSet_relabelRows`/`mem_XSet_relabelRows` and `mem_OSet_swapColumns`/`mem_XSet_swapColumns` in `Diagram.lean:473`-`550`; the new file has `GridState.mem_transpose_pointSet` at `Transpose.lean:126` but only `transpose_OSet`/`transpose_XSet` at `Transpose.lean:184`-`190` for diagrams.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` lemmas `p ∈ G.transpose.OSet ↔ Prod.swap p ∈ G.OSet` and `p ∈ G.transpose.XSet ↔ Prod.swap p ∈ G.XSet`, proved from `GridState.mem_transpose_pointSet` or the set equalities.
findings.issue: `GridDiagram.transpose` exposes only `transpose_OSet`/`transpose_XSet` set-image equalities, but no direct `mem_OSet_transpose` or `mem_XSet_transpose` iff lemmas for users to reason about reflected markings without unfolding/image witnesses.

summary: The state transpose API has the needed simp membership lemma, but the diagram transpose API omits the corresponding marking-set membership characterizations. This leaves the public API incomplete relative to the existing row/column symmetry interface.
